title: Seattle Argosy Cruises Review: Harbor and Locks Cruise with Traveling While Black
content:
Seattle Argosy Cruises Review: Harbor and Locks Cruise with Traveling While Black
TWB with Marlie and Anthony Love
21000 subscribers
76 likes
1532 views
19 Jun 2022
Episode 103:

Join us as embark on an exciting adventure with Argosy Cruises in Seattle. Experience the Harbor Cruise and the Locks Cruise through our eyes, with insights on the best features, tips for an enjoyable trip, and their personal experiences as Black travelers. This video is perfect for anyone planning a visit to Seattle and looking for an engaging, informative cruise experience. Dive into the beauty of Seattle's waterfront and learn what makes these cruises a must-try!

To get your tickets for an Argosy Cruise head here: https://www.argosycruises.com/cruise-types/sightseeing-cruises/.

Join our mailing list: https://mailchi.mp/d939aab86868/traveling-while-black-mailing-list

Be sure to check our interactive map if you’re visiting Seattle at travelingwhileblackseattle.com OR https://map.proxi.co/r/0udm3TJOdJGIW1TPKejh.

title: Day Trips to Nearby Islands - Visit Seattle
content: Best of all, they’re only a day trip from Seattle. A group of people stand at the front of a ferry boat, looking out over blue water toward a distant tree-lined shoreline and a city skyline under a clear, sunny sky. Bainbridge Island Ferry photo credit James Schwabel/Alamy Stock Photo. ### Bainbridge Island. The 35-minute trip to this island via **Washington State Ferries** has long been a must for glorious skyline views. After docking at the ferry terminal, dive right into nature at the renowned **Bloedel Reserve**, which includes 150 acres and a Japanese garden. Drive 40 minutes north to Mukilteo to make the 20-minute ferry ride to this long, curving island that’s dotted with cute villages and breezy beaches. This 172-island archipelago, which was named a national monument in 2013, is located 105 miles northwest of Seattle. It’s a 15-minute ferry hop from West Seattle to this small island with an agrarian vibe.
